name: Janis Siegel
abstract: Janis Siegel (born July 23, 1952) is a multiple grammy-winning American jazz singer, best known as a member of the vocal group The Manhattan Transfer.

interviews.title: Janis Siegel interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, May 22, 2015

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival: Modern Jazz Quartet 40th Anniversary Concert
JVC Jazz Festival: The Manhattan Transfer
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Jon Hendricks
about_quotes.evidence: That’s why I helped them. ... My name is Tim Hauser and my girlfriend Janis Siegel and I live in Brooklyn and we’re going to start a Lambert, Hendricks & Ross type group called Manhattan Transfer. I gave him my card, and I said, “any time I can help you, let me know.” And that was how they started.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon Hendricks interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 29, 2000 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Jane Monheit
about_quotes.evidence: It was Frank Vignola, Janis Siegel, and just a bunch of guys. And he was very close with all of us and we all loved him so much, and we all miss him so much that whenever we see each other we still talk about him every time.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jane Monheit interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, August 20, 2010
